channel.title: Recipe Workflows: Meal Planning, Shopping, and Cooking with Cooklang on Cooklang
channel.description: Recent content in Recipe Workflows: Meal Planning, Shopping, and Cooking with Cooklang on Cooklang

item.title: Version Control Your Recipes with Git
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Every recipe app has a 'what if the company dies?' problem. If your recipes are plain text .cook files in a Git repo, that problem disappears. Here's how to set up a proper recipe repo, track changes meaningfully, collaborate with family, and publish to the Cooklang Federation.

item.title: Cooklang for Food Bloggers: Write Once, Publish Everywhere
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: One .cook file can generate your blog post, Google-ready Schema.org markup, a printable PDF, and a shopping list — without reformatting anything by hand.

item.title: How to Scale a Recipe Up or Down (Without Ruining It)
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Scaling recipes sounds like simple math until your cookies come out flat or your soup is inedibly salty. Here's why scaling fails and how to do it right.

item.title: How to Save Recipes from Social Media and Keep Them Forever
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Recipes you save on Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook disappear when creators delete posts or platforms change. Here's a workflow to capture social media recipes and store them permanently as plain text files you control.

item.title: Recipe Discovery Without the Ads and Life Stories
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Modern recipe sites bury recipes under ads, pop-ups, and SEO filler. Cooklang Federation offers a different approach — a searchable index of community-tested recipes with no ads, no tracking, and no stories about someone's trip to Tuscany.

item.title: Building the Perfect Pantry with CookCLI: How the Greedy Coverage Algorithm Works
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: A practical deep dive into how CookCLI uses algorithmic coverage analysis to help users build the most efficient pantry for their recipe collection.

item.title: The Dishwasher Salmon Problem
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Recipe blogs prioritize ads over quality, creating bizarre dishes like dishwasher salmon. Cooklang Federation solves this by connecting you to tried and true community recipes.

item.title: Meal Planning as Compilation
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description:
I used to think meal planning was for people with too much time. Then I realized I was spending more time not planning meals than it would take to plan them.

Let me be clear: what I'm about to describe isn't fully built yet. It's how I want meal planning to work, how it should work. Some pieces exist in Cooklang today, others are still being developed. Think of this as a design document for the future of meal planning, not a manual for current software. (Since writing this, some of it is real: here's planning a week of dinners with AI from your own collection.)

item.title: The Pantry Problem
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description:
I realized something was wrong with how we manage food when I opened my third jar of cumin. Not because I love cumin that much, but because I'd forgotten about the other two.

This is embarrassing for someone who writes software. I've built systems that track millions of data points, but I couldn't track twelve spice jars. The problem wasn't intelligence or organization. It was that I was using the wrong tools for the job.

item.title: Automated Grocery List: Save Time and Money with Meal Planning
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Build an automated grocery list from your meal plan using Cooklang. Practical tips for shopping list automation, reducing food waste, and cutting your grocery bill.

item.title: How to Automate Grocery Shopping with Plain Text Recipes
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: How I automated my grocery shopping by creating Cooklang — a markup language that turns recipe files into shopping lists. From sticky notes to command-line automation.
